channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Farmers are changing their CSA models to meet consumers where they are
item.description: Community-supported agriculture delivers local produce by having customers pay a farm upfront. Some farmers are changing the model to appeal to consumer preferences.
item.content:encoded: Community-supported agriculture delivers local produce by having customers pay a farm upfront. Some farmers are changing the model to appeal to consumer preferences.
item.dc:creator: Hope Kirwan

item.title: Trump says deal has been reached to end the war with Iran
item.description: On social media, President Trump says a deal between Washington and Tehran to end the war "is now complete." He says the deal will be signed Friday.
item.content:encoded: On social media, President Trump says a deal between Washington and Tehran to end the war "is now complete." He says the deal will be signed Friday.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Iran expert Karim Sadjadpour on ceasefire deal to end Iran war
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Karim Sadjadpour, of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, about a ceasefire deal with Iran, announced by President Trump and Pakistani mediators.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Karim Sadjadpour, of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, about a ceasefire deal with Iran, announced by President Trump and Pakistani mediators.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Most parents track their 18- to 25-year-old kids on their smartphones. Is it healthy?
item.description: A new survey from the University of Michigan asks parents about their use of technology to track their adult children, ages 18-25, including using "always on" location tracking on their smartphones.
item.content:encoded: A new survey from the University of Michigan asks parents about their use of technology to track their adult children, ages 18-25, including using "always on" location tracking on their smartphones.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Inside Ebola country: NPR reports from eastern DR Congo's outbreak zone
item.description: In eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, an Ebola outbreak is reshaping life, with fear spreading faster than information and hospitals turning into places of urgent containment rather than care.
item.content:encoded:
In eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, an Ebola outbreak is reshaping life, with fear spreading faster than information and hospitals turning into places of urgent containment rather than care.

(Image credit: Arsène Mpiana Monkwe for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: President Trump says a deal has been reached to end the Iran war, Trump celebrates birthday with UFC event, G7 summit kicks off Monday amid tensions between Europe and U.S.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says a deal has been reached to end the Iran war, Trump celebrates birthday with UFC event, G7 summit kicks off Monday amid tensions between Europe and U.S.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: 12 dead after skydiving plane crashes in Missouri
item.description: Authorities say 11 skydivers and a pilot are presumed dead after a single-engine turboprop plane crashed Sunday near Butler, Missouri.
item.content:encoded: Authorities say 11 skydivers and a pilot are presumed dead after a single-engine turboprop plane crashed Sunday near Butler, Missouri.
item.dc:creator: Madeline Fox

item.title: Crude oil futures drop after Trump promises an Iran deal will be signed Friday
item.description: The price of crude oil dropped shortly after President Trump announced that the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a deal that, when signed Friday, would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: The price of crude oil dropped shortly after President Trump announced that the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a deal that, when signed Friday, would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Iran's World Cup game brings mixed emotions for Iranian Americans living in L.A.
item.description: Iran's national soccer team will play against New Zealand Monday night in Los Angeles, home to the largest Iranian community outside Iran. Many are torn on whether to support Iran's team.
item.content:encoded: Iran's national soccer team will play against New Zealand Monday night in Los Angeles, home to the largest Iranian community outside Iran. Many are torn on whether to support Iran's team.
item.dc:creator: Arezou Rezvani

item.title: Elite Ukrainian military unit uses drones to weaken Kremlin's war machine
item.description: NPR joins a Ukrainian military unit that launches long-range drones deep inside Russia, including Moscow, in an effort to weaken Russia's war machine.
item.content:encoded: NPR joins a Ukrainian military unit that launches long-range drones deep inside Russia, including Moscow, in an effort to weaken Russia's war machine.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis
